episode_name: First Battle of Fallujah / Operation Vigilant Resolve
geographic_scope: Subnational, city-level: Fallujah city proper with immediate military cordon positions
theater_description: Urban siege and assault in Fallujah city. Civilian exposure is restricted to people physically present inside the city. Military exposure includes insurgents inside the city and Coalition/Iraqi forces directly assigned to the cordon and assaults.
geometry_method_note: Use the 2004 Fallujah urban footprint for civilian exposure. Do not extend the civilian denominator to Ramadi or wider al-Anbar. Include immediate blocking positions and attack bases only in the military denominator.
military_area_note: Combined military denominator includes U.S. Marines and attached units, Iraqi commandos/CDC forces actually participating, and insurgent fighters operating in or directly around the city.
civilian_area_note: Civilian denominator includes only civilians present inside Fallujah city during 5-30 April, adjusted downward for outflow after warnings and temporary pauses.
split_reason: Distinct offensive/ceasefire pattern, distinct source base, and very different civilian-presence dynamics from the November-December operation.
quality_note: Civilian death source is relatively transparent, but combatant deaths partly rely on case-specific allocation from the same total, and both person-month denominators are model-generated.

episode_name: Interbattle insurgent sanctuary and shaping phase
geographic_scope: Subnational, mixed city/corridor: Fallujah city and adjacent Fallujah-Ramadi corridor
theater_description: Period after the April halt and before the November assault, including the Fallujah Brigade episode, insurgent consolidation, precision strikes, and isolation/shaping operations.
geometry_method_note: Geometry changes materially during this phase as control, civilian return, and military action shift between city proper and wider approaches/corridor.
military_area_note: Military exposure would need corridor-sensitive reconstruction because shaping operations extended beyond the city proper.
civilian_area_note: Civilian exposure likely rose after the April siege and then fell again before the November assault; a single average denominator would be misleading.
split_reason: Theater, governance, and violence mode changed materially after the April battle and before al-Fajr; keeping this as a separate episode avoids contaminating the two main assault windows.
quality_note: Listed for periodization only. No full DR estimate completed in this bundle because city-scale civilian presence and direct-death numerators are not coherent enough without a separate dedicated reconstruction.

episode_name: Second Battle of Fallujah / Operation al-Fajr (Phantom Fury)
geographic_scope: Subnational, city-level: Fallujah city proper with isolation belt and direct assault positions
theater_description: Joint U.S.-Iraqi assault preceded by isolation and civilian flight. Civilian exposure is restricted to civilians still physically present in the city; military exposure includes insurgents in the city and Coalition/Iraqi forces directly assigned to isolation and urban clearing.
geometry_method_note: Use city proper for civilian exposure. Include immediate sealing/approach-security positions in the military denominator because they were directly assigned to the assault theater. End the civilian-denominator window on 23 December because reintroduction of civilians began on 24 December.
military_area_note: Combined military denominator includes Coalition/Iraqi units directly involved in assault or isolation and insurgent forces defending the city.
civilian_area_note: Civilian denominator excludes the broader 300,000-city baseline once residents fled; exposure is reduced sharply using presence scenarios bounded by competing contemporaneous claims.
split_reason: Different belligerent mix, much larger assault force, major pre-assault civilian exodus, different casualty reporting architecture, and a more defensible closed-city exposure window than in April.
quality_note: Military numerators are better documented than in April, but civilian deaths remain incomplete/provisional and the number of civilians still in the city at assault start is sharply disputed.
